2191 VPValue *getSplatVFValue() {
2192 // If the recipe has been unrolled (4 operands), return the VPValue for the
2193 // induction increment.
2194 return getNumOperands() == 5 ? getOperand(3) : nullptr;
2195 }
2196
2197 /// Returns the first defined value as TruncInst, if it is one or nullptr
2198 /// otherwise.
2199 TruncInst *getTruncInst() { return Trunc; }
2200 const TruncInst *getTruncInst() const { return Trunc; }
2201
2202 /// Returns true if the induction is canonical, i.e. starting at 0 and
2203 /// incremented by UF * VF (= the original IV is incremented by 1) and has the
2204 /// same type as the canonical induction.
2205 bool isCanonical() const;
2206
2207 /// Returns the scalar type of the induction.
2208 Type *getScalarType() const {
2209 return Trunc ? Trunc->getType() : getPHINode()->getType();
2210 }
2211
2212 /// Returns the VPValue representing the value of this induction at
2213 /// the last unrolled part, if it exists. Returns itself if unrolling did not
2214 /// take place.
2215 VPValue *getLastUnrolledPartOperand() {
2216 return getNumOperands() == 5 ? getOperand(4) : this;
2217 }
2218};
2219
2220class VPWidenPointerInductionRecipe : public VPWidenInductionRecipe,
2221 public VPUnrollPartAccessor<3> {
2222 bool IsScalarAfterVectorization;
2223
2224public:
2225 /// Create a new VPWidenPointerInductionRecipe for \p Phi with start value \p
2226 /// Start.
2227 VPWidenPointerInductionRecipe(PHINode *Phi, VPValue *Start, VPValue *Step,
2228 const InductionDescriptor &IndDesc,
2229 bool IsScalarAfterVectorization, DebugLoc DL)
2230 : VPWidenInductionRecipe(VPDef::VPWidenPointerInductionSC, Phi, Start,
2231 Step, IndDesc, DL),
2232 IsScalarAfterVectorization(IsScalarAfterVectorization) {}
2233
2234 ~VPWidenPointerInductionRecipe() override = default;
2235
2236 VPWidenPointerInductionRecipe *clone() override {
2237 return new VPWidenPointerInductionRecipe(
2238 cast<PHINode>(getUnderlyingInstr()), getOperand(0), getOperand(1),
2239 getInductionDescriptor(), IsScalarAfterVectorization, getDebugLoc());
2240 }
2241
2242 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPWidenPointerInductionSC)
2243
2244 /// Generate vector values for the pointer induction.
2245 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2246
2247 /// Returns true if only scalar values will be generated.
2248 bool onlyScalarsGenerated(bool IsScalable);
2249
2250 /// Returns the VPValue representing the value of this induction at
2251 /// the first unrolled part, if it exists. Returns itself if unrolling did not
2252 /// take place.
2253 VPValue *getFirstUnrolledPartOperand() {
2254 return getUnrollPart(*this) == 0 ? this : getOperand(2);
2255 }
2256
2257#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2258 /// Print the recipe.
2259 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2260 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2261#endif
2262};
2263
2264/// Recipe to generate a scalar PHI. Used to generate code for recipes that
2265/// produce scalar header phis, including VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe and
2266/// VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe.
2267class VPScalarPHIRecipe : public VPHeaderPHIRecipe {
2268 std::string Name;
2269
2270public:
2271 VPScalarPHIRecipe(VPValue *Start, VPValue *BackedgeValue, DebugLoc DL,
2272 StringRef Name)
2273 : VPHeaderPHIRecipe(VPDef::VPScalarPHISC, nullptr, Start, DL),
2274 Name(Name.str()) {
2275 addOperand(BackedgeValue);
2276 }
2277
2278 ~VPScalarPHIRecipe() override = default;
2279
2280 VPScalarPHIRecipe *clone() override {
2281 llvm_unreachable("cloning not implemented yet");
2282 }
2283
2284 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPScalarPHISC)
2285
2286 /// Generate the phi/select nodes.
2287 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2288
2289 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
2290 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
2291 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
2292 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
2293 return true;
2294 }
2295
2296#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2297 /// Print the recipe.
2298 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2299 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2300#endif
2301};
2302
2303/// A recipe for handling phis that are widened in the vector loop.
2304/// In the VPlan native path, all incoming VPValues & VPBasicBlock pairs are
2305/// managed in the recipe directly.
2306class VPWidenPHIRecipe : public VPSingleDefRecipe {
2307 /// List of incoming blocks. Only used in the VPlan native path.
2308 SmallVector<VPBasicBlock *, 2> IncomingBlocks;
2309
2310public:
2311 /// Create a new VPWidenPHIRecipe for \p Phi with start value \p Start and
2312 /// debug location \p DL.
2313 VPWidenPHIRecipe(PHINode *Phi, VPValue *Start = nullptr, DebugLoc DL = {})
2314 : VPSingleDefRecipe(VPDef::VPWidenPHISC, ArrayRef<VPValue *>(), Phi, DL) {
2315 if (Start)
2316 addOperand(Start);
2317 }
2318
2319 VPWidenPHIRecipe *clone() override {
2320 llvm_unreachable("cloning not implemented yet");
2321 }
2322
2323 ~VPWidenPHIRecipe() override = default;
2324
2325 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPWidenPHISC)
2326
2327 /// Generate the phi/select nodes.
2328 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2329
2330#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2331 /// Print the recipe.
2332 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2333 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2334#endif
2335
2336 /// Adds a pair (\p IncomingV, \p IncomingBlock) to the phi.
2337 void addIncoming(VPValue *IncomingV, VPBasicBlock *IncomingBlock) {
2338 addOperand(IncomingV);
2339 IncomingBlocks.push_back(IncomingBlock);
2340 }
2341
2342 /// Returns the \p I th incoming VPBasicBlock.
2343 VPBasicBlock *getIncomingBlock(unsigned I) { return IncomingBlocks[I]; }
2344
2345 /// Returns the \p I th incoming VPValue.
2346 VPValue *getIncomingValue(unsigned I) { return getOperand(I); }
2347};
2348
2349/// A recipe for handling first-order recurrence phis. The start value is the
2350/// first operand of the recipe and the incoming value from the backedge is the
2351/// second operand.
2352struct VPFirstOrderRecurrencePHIRecipe : public VPHeaderPHIRecipe {
2353 VPFirstOrderRecurrencePHIRecipe(PHINode *Phi, VPValue &Start)
2354 : VPHeaderPHIRecipe(VPDef::VPFirstOrderRecurrencePHISC, Phi, &Start) {}
2355
2356 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPFirstOrderRecurrencePHISC)
2357
2358 static inline bool classof(const VPHeaderPHIRecipe *R) {
2359 return R->getVPDefID() == VPDef::VPFirstOrderRecurrencePHISC;
2360 }
2361
2362 VPFirstOrderRecurrencePHIRecipe *clone() override {
2363 return new VPFirstOrderRecurrencePHIRecipe(
2364 cast<PHINode>(getUnderlyingInstr()), *getOperand(0));
2365 }
2366
2367 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2368
2369 /// Return the cost of this first-order recurrence phi recipe.
2370 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
2371 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
2372
2373#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2374 /// Print the recipe.
2375 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2376 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2377#endif
2378};
2379
2380/// A recipe for handling reduction phis. The start value is the first operand
2381/// of the recipe and the incoming value from the backedge is the second
2382/// operand.
2383class VPReductionPHIRecipe : public VPHeaderPHIRecipe,
2384 public VPUnrollPartAccessor<2> {
2385 /// Descriptor for the reduction.
2386 const RecurrenceDescriptor &RdxDesc;
2387
2388 /// The phi is part of an in-loop reduction.
2389 bool IsInLoop;
2390
2391 /// The phi is part of an ordered reduction. Requires IsInLoop to be true.
2392 bool IsOrdered;
2393
2394 /// When expanding the reduction PHI, the plan's VF element count is divided
2395 /// by this factor to form the reduction phi's VF.
2396 unsigned VFScaleFactor = 1;
2397
2398public:
2399 /// Create a new VPReductionPHIRecipe for the reduction \p Phi described by \p
2400 /// RdxDesc.
2401 VPReductionPHIRecipe(PHINode *Phi, const RecurrenceDescriptor &RdxDesc,
2402 VPValue &Start, bool IsInLoop = false,
2403 bool IsOrdered = false, unsigned VFScaleFactor = 1)
2404 : VPHeaderPHIRecipe(VPDef::VPReductionPHISC, Phi, &Start),
2405 RdxDesc(RdxDesc), IsInLoop(IsInLoop), IsOrdered(IsOrdered),
2406 VFScaleFactor(VFScaleFactor) {
2407 assert((!IsOrdered || IsInLoop) && "IsOrdered requires IsInLoop");
2408 }
2409
2410 ~VPReductionPHIRecipe() override = default;
2411
2412 VPReductionPHIRecipe *clone() override {
2413 auto *R = new VPReductionPHIRecipe(cast<PHINode>(getUnderlyingInstr()),
2414 RdxDesc, *getOperand(0), IsInLoop,
2415 IsOrdered, VFScaleFactor);
2416 R->addOperand(getBackedgeValue());
2417 return R;
2418 }
2419
2420 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPReductionPHISC)
2421
2422 static inline bool classof(const VPHeaderPHIRecipe *R) {
2423 return R->getVPDefID() == VPDef::VPReductionPHISC;
2424 }
2425
2426 /// Generate the phi/select nodes.
2427 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2428
2429#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2430 /// Print the recipe.
2431 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2432 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2433#endif
2434
2435 const RecurrenceDescriptor &getRecurrenceDescriptor() const {
2436 return RdxDesc;
2437 }
2438
2439 /// Returns true, if the phi is part of an ordered reduction.
2440 bool isOrdered() const { return IsOrdered; }
2441
2442 /// Returns true, if the phi is part of an in-loop reduction.
2443 bool isInLoop() const { return IsInLoop; }
2444};
2445
2446/// A recipe for forming partial reductions. In the loop, an accumulator and
2447/// vector operand are added together and passed to the next iteration as the
2448/// next accumulator. After the loop body, the accumulator is reduced to a
2449/// scalar value.
2450class VPPartialReductionRecipe : public VPSingleDefRecipe {
2451 unsigned Opcode;
2452
2453public:
2454 VPPartialReductionRecipe(Instruction *ReductionInst, VPValue *Op0,
2455 VPValue *Op1)
2456 : VPPartialReductionRecipe(ReductionInst->getOpcode(), Op0, Op1,
2457 ReductionInst) {}
2458 VPPartialReductionRecipe(unsigned Opcode, VPValue *Op0, VPValue *Op1,
2459 Instruction *ReductionInst = nullptr)
2460 : VPSingleDefRecipe(VPDef::VPPartialReductionSC,
2461 ArrayRef<VPValue *>({Op0, Op1}), ReductionInst),
2462 Opcode(Opcode) {
2463 [[maybe_unused]] auto *AccumulatorRecipe =
2464 getOperand(1)->getDefiningRecipe();
2465 assert((isa<VPReductionPHIRecipe>(AccumulatorRecipe) ||
2466 isa<VPPartialReductionRecipe>(AccumulatorRecipe)) &&
2467 "Unexpected operand order for partial reduction recipe");
2468 }
2469 ~VPPartialReductionRecipe() override = default;
2470
2471 VPPartialReductionRecipe *clone() override {
2472 return new VPPartialReductionRecipe(Opcode, getOperand(0), getOperand(1),
2473 getUnderlyingInstr());
2474 }
2475
2476 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPPartialReductionSC)
2477
2478 /// Generate the reduction in the loop.
2479 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2480
2481 /// Return the cost of this VPPartialReductionRecipe.
2482 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
2483 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
2484
2485 /// Get the binary op's opcode.
2486 unsigned getOpcode() const { return Opcode; }
2487
2488#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2489 /// Print the recipe.
2490 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2491 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2492#endif
2493};
2494
2495/// A recipe for vectorizing a phi-node as a sequence of mask-based select
2496/// instructions.
2497class VPBlendRecipe : public VPSingleDefRecipe {
2498public:
2499 /// The blend operation is a User of the incoming values and of their
2500 /// respective masks, ordered [I0, M0, I1, M1, I2, M2, ...]. Note that M0 can
2501 /// be omitted (implied by passing an odd number of operands) in which case
2502 /// all other incoming values are merged into it.
2503 VPBlendRecipe(PHINode *Phi, ArrayRef<VPValue *> Operands)
2504 : VPSingleDefRecipe(VPDef::VPBlendSC, Operands, Phi, Phi->getDebugLoc()) {
2505 assert(Operands.size() > 0 && "Expected at least one operand!");
2506 }
2507
2508 VPBlendRecipe *clone() override {
2509 SmallVector<VPValue *> Ops(operands());
2510 return new VPBlendRecipe(cast<PHINode>(getUnderlyingValue()), Ops);
2511 }
2512
2513 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPBlendSC)
2514
2515 /// A normalized blend is one that has an odd number of operands, whereby the
2516 /// first operand does not have an associated mask.
2517 bool isNormalized() const { return getNumOperands() % 2; }
2518
2519 /// Return the number of incoming values, taking into account when normalized
2520 /// the first incoming value will have no mask.
2521 unsigned getNumIncomingValues() const {
2522 return (getNumOperands() + isNormalized()) / 2;
2523 }
2524
2525 /// Return incoming value number \p Idx.
2526 VPValue *getIncomingValue(unsigned Idx) const {
2527 return Idx == 0 ? getOperand(0) : getOperand(Idx * 2 - isNormalized());
2528 }
2529
2530 /// Return mask number \p Idx.
2531 VPValue *getMask(unsigned Idx) const {
2532 assert((Idx > 0 || !isNormalized()) && "First index has no mask!");
2533 return Idx == 0 ? getOperand(1) : getOperand(Idx * 2 + !isNormalized());
2534 }
2535
2536 /// Generate the phi/select nodes.
2537 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2538
2539 /// Return the cost of this VPWidenMemoryRecipe.
2540 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
2541 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
2542
2543#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2544 /// Print the recipe.
2545 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2546 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2547#endif
2548
2549 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
2550 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
2551 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
2552 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
2553 // Recursing through Blend recipes only, must terminate at header phi's the
2554 // latest.
2555 return all_of(users(),
2556 [this](VPUser *U) { return U->onlyFirstLaneUsed(this); });
2557 }
2558};
2559
2560/// VPInterleaveRecipe is a recipe for transforming an interleave group of load
2561/// or stores into one wide load/store and shuffles. The first operand of a
2562/// VPInterleave recipe is the address, followed by the stored values, followed
2563/// by an optional mask.
2564class VPInterleaveRecipe : public VPRecipeBase {
2565 const InterleaveGroup<Instruction> *IG;
2566
2567 /// Indicates if the interleave group is in a conditional block and requires a
2568 /// mask.
2569 bool HasMask = false;
2570
2571 /// Indicates if gaps between members of the group need to be masked out or if
2572 /// unusued gaps can be loaded speculatively.
2573 bool NeedsMaskForGaps = false;
2574
2575public:
2576 VPInterleaveRecipe(const InterleaveGroup<Instruction> *IG, VPValue *Addr,
2577 ArrayRef<VPValue *> StoredValues, VPValue *Mask,
2578 bool NeedsMaskForGaps)
2579 : VPRecipeBase(VPDef::VPInterleaveSC, {Addr}), IG(IG),
2580 NeedsMaskForGaps(NeedsMaskForGaps) {
2581 for (unsigned i = 0; i < IG->getFactor(); ++i)
2582 if (Instruction *I = IG->getMember(i)) {
2583 if (I->getType()->isVoidTy())
2584 continue;
2585 new VPValue(I, this);
2586 }
2587
2588 for (auto *SV : StoredValues)
2589 addOperand(SV);
2590 if (Mask) {
2591 HasMask = true;
2592 addOperand(Mask);
2593 }
2594 }
2595 ~VPInterleaveRecipe() override = default;
2596
2597 VPInterleaveRecipe *clone() override {
2598 return new VPInterleaveRecipe(IG, getAddr(), getStoredValues(), getMask(),
2599 NeedsMaskForGaps);
2600 }
2601
2602 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPInterleaveSC)
2603
2604 /// Return the address accessed by this recipe.
2605 VPValue *getAddr() const {
2606 return getOperand(0); // Address is the 1st, mandatory operand.
2607 }
2608
2609 /// Return the mask used by this recipe. Note that a full mask is represented
2610 /// by a nullptr.
2611 VPValue *getMask() const {
2612 // Mask is optional and therefore the last, currently 2nd operand.
2613 return HasMask ? getOperand(getNumOperands() - 1) : nullptr;
2614 }
2615
2616 /// Return the VPValues stored by this interleave group. If it is a load
2617 /// interleave group, return an empty ArrayRef.
2618 ArrayRef<VPValue *> getStoredValues() const {
2619 // The first operand is the address, followed by the stored values, followed
2620 // by an optional mask.
2621 return ArrayRef<VPValue *>(op_begin(), getNumOperands())
2622 .slice(1, getNumStoreOperands());
2623 }
2624
2625 /// Generate the wide load or store, and shuffles.
2626 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2627
2628 /// Return the cost of this VPInterleaveRecipe.
2629 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
2630 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
2631
2632#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2633 /// Print the recipe.
2634 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2635 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2636#endif
2637
2638 const InterleaveGroup<Instruction> *getInterleaveGroup() { return IG; }
2639
2640 /// Returns the number of stored operands of this interleave group. Returns 0
2641 /// for load interleave groups.
2642 unsigned getNumStoreOperands() const {
2643 return getNumOperands() - (HasMask ? 2 : 1);
2644 }
2645
2646 /// The recipe only uses the first lane of the address.
2647 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
2648 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
2649 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
2650 return Op == getAddr() && !llvm::is_contained(getStoredValues(), Op);
2651 }
2652
2653 Instruction *getInsertPos() const { return IG->getInsertPos(); }
2654};
2655
2656/// A recipe to represent inloop reduction operations, performing a reduction on
2657/// a vector operand into a scalar value, and adding the result to a chain.
2658/// The Operands are {ChainOp, VecOp, [Condition]}.
2659class VPReductionRecipe : public VPSingleDefRecipe {
2660 /// The recurrence decriptor for the reduction in question.
2661 const RecurrenceDescriptor &RdxDesc;
2662 bool IsOrdered;
2663 /// Whether the reduction is conditional.
2664 bool IsConditional = false;
2665
2666protected:
2667 VPReductionRecipe(const unsigned char SC, const RecurrenceDescriptor &R,
2668 Instruction *I, ArrayRef<VPValue *> Operands,
2669 VPValue *CondOp, bool IsOrdered, DebugLoc DL)
2670 : VPSingleDefRecipe(SC, Operands, I, DL), RdxDesc(R),
2671 IsOrdered(IsOrdered) {
2672 if (CondOp) {
2673 IsConditional = true;
2674 addOperand(CondOp);
2675 }
2676 }
2677
2678public:
2679 VPReductionRecipe(const RecurrenceDescriptor &R, Instruction *I,
2680 VPValue *ChainOp, VPValue *VecOp, VPValue *CondOp,
2681 bool IsOrdered, DebugLoc DL = {})
2682 : VPReductionRecipe(VPDef::VPReductionSC, R, I,
2683 ArrayRef<VPValue *>({ChainOp, VecOp}), CondOp,
2684 IsOrdered, DL) {}
2685
2686 ~VPReductionRecipe() override = default;
2687
2688 VPReductionRecipe *clone() override {
2689 return new VPReductionRecipe(RdxDesc, getUnderlyingInstr(), getChainOp(),
2690 getVecOp(), getCondOp(), IsOrdered,
2691 getDebugLoc());
2692 }
2693
2694 static inline bool classof(const VPRecipeBase *R) {
2695 return R->getVPDefID() == VPRecipeBase::VPReductionSC ||
2696 R->getVPDefID() == VPRecipeBase::VPReductionEVLSC;
2697 }
2698
2699 static inline bool classof(const VPUser *U) {
2700 auto *R = dyn_cast<VPRecipeBase>(U);
2701 return R && classof(R);
2702 }
2703
2704 /// Generate the reduction in the loop.
2705 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2706
2707 /// Return the cost of VPReductionRecipe.
2708 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
2709 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
2710
2711#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2712 /// Print the recipe.
2713 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2714 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2715#endif
2716
2717 /// Return the recurrence decriptor for the in-loop reduction.
2718 const RecurrenceDescriptor &getRecurrenceDescriptor() const {
2719 return RdxDesc;
2720 }
2721 /// Return true if the in-loop reduction is ordered.
2722 bool isOrdered() const { return IsOrdered; };
2723 /// Return true if the in-loop reduction is conditional.
2724 bool isConditional() const { return IsConditional; };
2725 /// The VPValue of the scalar Chain being accumulated.
2726 VPValue *getChainOp() const { return getOperand(0); }
2727 /// The VPValue of the vector value to be reduced.
2728 VPValue *getVecOp() const { return getOperand(1); }
2729 /// The VPValue of the condition for the block.
2730 VPValue *getCondOp() const {
2731 return isConditional() ? getOperand(getNumOperands() - 1) : nullptr;
2732 }
2733};
2734
2735/// A recipe to represent inloop reduction operations with vector-predication
2736/// intrinsics, performing a reduction on a vector operand with the explicit
2737/// vector length (EVL) into a scalar value, and adding the result to a chain.
2738/// The Operands are {ChainOp, VecOp, EVL, [Condition]}.
2739class VPReductionEVLRecipe : public VPReductionRecipe {
2740public:
2741 VPReductionEVLRecipe(VPReductionRecipe &R, VPValue &EVL, VPValue *CondOp)
2742 : VPReductionRecipe(
2743 VPDef::VPReductionEVLSC, R.getRecurrenceDescriptor(),
2744 cast_or_null<Instruction>(R.getUnderlyingValue()),
2745 ArrayRef<VPValue *>({R.getChainOp(), R.getVecOp(), &EVL}), CondOp,
2746 R.isOrdered(), R.getDebugLoc()) {}
2747
2748 ~VPReductionEVLRecipe() override = default;
2749
2750 VPReductionEVLRecipe *clone() override {
2751 llvm_unreachable("cloning not implemented yet");
2752 }
2753
2754 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPReductionEVLSC)
2755
2756 /// Generate the reduction in the loop
2757 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2758
2759#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2760 /// Print the recipe.
2761 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2762 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2763#endif
2764
2765 /// The VPValue of the explicit vector length.
2766 VPValue *getEVL() const { return getOperand(2); }
2767
2768 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
2769 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
2770 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
2771 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
2772 return Op == getEVL();
2773 }
2774};
2775
2776/// VPReplicateRecipe replicates a given instruction producing multiple scalar
2777/// copies of the original scalar type, one per lane, instead of producing a
2778/// single copy of widened type for all lanes. If the instruction is known to be
2779/// uniform only one copy, per lane zero, will be generated.
2780class VPReplicateRecipe : public VPRecipeWithIRFlags {
2781 /// Indicator if only a single replica per lane is needed.
2782 bool IsUniform;
2783
2784 /// Indicator if the replicas are also predicated.
2785 bool IsPredicated;
2786
2787public:
2788 template <typename IterT>
2789 VPReplicateRecipe(Instruction *I, iterator_range<IterT> Operands,
2790 bool IsUniform, VPValue *Mask = nullptr)
2791 : VPRecipeWithIRFlags(VPDef::VPReplicateSC, Operands, *I),
2792 IsUniform(IsUniform), IsPredicated(Mask) {
2793 if (Mask)
2794 addOperand(Mask);
2795 }
2796
2797 ~VPReplicateRecipe() override = default;
2798
2799 VPReplicateRecipe *clone() override {
2800 auto *Copy =
2801 new VPReplicateRecipe(getUnderlyingInstr(), operands(), IsUniform,
2802 isPredicated() ? getMask() : nullptr);
2803 Copy->transferFlags(*this);
2804 return Copy;
2805 }
2806
2807 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPReplicateSC)
2808
2809 /// Generate replicas of the desired Ingredient. Replicas will be generated
2810 /// for all parts and lanes unless a specific part and lane are specified in
2811 /// the \p State.
2812 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2813
2814 /// Return the cost of this VPReplicateRecipe.
2815 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
2816 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
2817
2818#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2819 /// Print the recipe.
2820 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2821 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2822#endif
2823
2824 bool isUniform() const { return IsUniform; }
2825
2826 bool isPredicated() const { return IsPredicated; }
2827
2828 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
2829 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
2830 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
2831 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
2832 return isUniform();
2833 }
2834
2835 /// Returns true if the recipe uses scalars of operand \p Op.
2836 bool usesScalars(const VPValue *Op) const override {
2837 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
2838 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
2839 return true;
2840 }
2841
2842 /// Returns true if the recipe is used by a widened recipe via an intervening
2843 /// VPPredInstPHIRecipe. In this case, the scalar values should also be packed
2844 /// in a vector.
2845 bool shouldPack() const;
2846
2847 /// Return the mask of a predicated VPReplicateRecipe.
2848 VPValue *getMask() {
2849 assert(isPredicated() && "Trying to get the mask of a unpredicated recipe");
2850 return getOperand(getNumOperands() - 1);
2851 }
2852
2853 unsigned getOpcode() const { return getUnderlyingInstr()->getOpcode(); }
2854};
2855
2856/// A recipe for generating conditional branches on the bits of a mask.
2857class VPBranchOnMaskRecipe : public VPRecipeBase {
2858public:
2859 VPBranchOnMaskRecipe(VPValue *BlockInMask)
2860 : VPRecipeBase(VPDef::VPBranchOnMaskSC, {}) {
2861 if (BlockInMask) // nullptr means all-one mask.
2862 addOperand(BlockInMask);
2863 }
2864
2865 VPBranchOnMaskRecipe *clone() override {
2866 return new VPBranchOnMaskRecipe(getOperand(0));
2867 }
2868
2869 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPBranchOnMaskSC)
2870
2871 /// Generate the extraction of the appropriate bit from the block mask and the
2872 /// conditional branch.
2873 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2874
2875 /// Return the cost of this VPBranchOnMaskRecipe.
2876 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
2877 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
2878
2879#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2880 /// Print the recipe.
2881 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2882 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override {
2883 O << Indent << "BRANCH-ON-MASK ";
2884 if (VPValue *Mask = getMask())
2885 Mask->printAsOperand(O, SlotTracker);
2886 else
2887 O << " All-One";
2888 }
2889#endif
2890
2891 /// Return the mask used by this recipe. Note that a full mask is represented
2892 /// by a nullptr.
2893 VPValue *getMask() const {
2894 assert(getNumOperands() <= 1 && "should have either 0 or 1 operands");
2895 // Mask is optional.
2896 return getNumOperands() == 1 ? getOperand(0) : nullptr;
2897 }
2898
2899 /// Returns true if the recipe uses scalars of operand \p Op.
2900 bool usesScalars(const VPValue *Op) const override {
2901 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
2902 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
2903 return true;
2904 }
2905};
2906
2907/// VPPredInstPHIRecipe is a recipe for generating the phi nodes needed when
2908/// control converges back from a Branch-on-Mask. The phi nodes are needed in
2909/// order to merge values that are set under such a branch and feed their uses.
2910/// The phi nodes can be scalar or vector depending on the users of the value.
2911/// This recipe works in concert with VPBranchOnMaskRecipe.
2912class VPPredInstPHIRecipe : public VPSingleDefRecipe {
2913public:
2914 /// Construct a VPPredInstPHIRecipe given \p PredInst whose value needs a phi
2915 /// nodes after merging back from a Branch-on-Mask.
2916 VPPredInstPHIRecipe(VPValue *PredV, DebugLoc DL)
2917 : VPSingleDefRecipe(VPDef::VPPredInstPHISC, PredV, DL) {}
2918 ~VPPredInstPHIRecipe() override = default;
2919
2920 VPPredInstPHIRecipe *clone() override {
2921 return new VPPredInstPHIRecipe(getOperand(0), getDebugLoc());
2922 }
2923
2924 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPPredInstPHISC)
2925
2926 /// Generates phi nodes for live-outs (from a replicate region) as needed to
2927 /// retain SSA form.
2928 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
2929
2930 /// Return the cost of this VPPredInstPHIRecipe.
2931 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
2932 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override {
2933 // TODO: Compute accurate cost after retiring the legacy cost model.
2934 return 0;
2935 }
2936
2937#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
2938 /// Print the recipe.
2939 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
2940 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
2941#endif
2942
2943 /// Returns true if the recipe uses scalars of operand \p Op.
2944 bool usesScalars(const VPValue *Op) const override {
2945 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
2946 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
2947 return true;
2948 }
2949};
2950
2951/// A common base class for widening memory operations. An optional mask can be
2952/// provided as the last operand.
2953class VPWidenMemoryRecipe : public VPRecipeBase {
2954protected:
2955 Instruction &Ingredient;
2956
2957 /// Whether the accessed addresses are consecutive.
2958 bool Consecutive;
2959
2960 /// Whether the consecutive accessed addresses are in reverse order.
2961 bool Reverse;
2962
2963 /// Whether the memory access is masked.
2964 bool IsMasked = false;
2965
2966 void setMask(VPValue *Mask) {
2967 assert(!IsMasked && "cannot re-set mask");
2968 if (!Mask)
2969 return;
2970 addOperand(Mask);
2971 IsMasked = true;
2972 }
2973
2974 VPWidenMemoryRecipe(const char unsigned SC, Instruction &I,
2975 std::initializer_list<VPValue *> Operands,
2976 bool Consecutive, bool Reverse, DebugLoc DL)
2977 : VPRecipeBase(SC, Operands, DL), Ingredient(I), Consecutive(Consecutive),
2978 Reverse(Reverse) {
2979 assert((Consecutive || !Reverse) && "Reverse implies consecutive");
2980 }
2981
2982public:
2983 VPWidenMemoryRecipe *clone() override {
2984 llvm_unreachable("cloning not supported");
2985 }
2986
2987 static inline bool classof(const VPRecipeBase *R) {
2988 return R->getVPDefID() == VPRecipeBase::VPWidenLoadSC ||
2989 R->getVPDefID() == VPRecipeBase::VPWidenStoreSC ||
2990 R->getVPDefID() == VPRecipeBase::VPWidenLoadEVLSC ||
2991 R->getVPDefID() == VPRecipeBase::VPWidenStoreEVLSC;
2992 }
2993
2994 static inline bool classof(const VPUser *U) {
2995 auto *R = dyn_cast<VPRecipeBase>(U);
2996 return R && classof(R);
2997 }
2998
2999 /// Return whether the loaded-from / stored-to addresses are consecutive.
3000 bool isConsecutive() const { return Consecutive; }
3001
3002 /// Return whether the consecutive loaded/stored addresses are in reverse
3003 /// order.
3004 bool isReverse() const { return Reverse; }
3005
3006 /// Return the address accessed by this recipe.
3007 VPValue *getAddr() const { return getOperand(0); }
3008
3009 /// Returns true if the recipe is masked.
3010 bool isMasked() const { return IsMasked; }
3011
3012 /// Return the mask used by this recipe. Note that a full mask is represented
3013 /// by a nullptr.
3014 VPValue *getMask() const {
3015 // Mask is optional and therefore the last operand.
3016 return isMasked() ? getOperand(getNumOperands() - 1) : nullptr;
3017 }
3018
3019 /// Generate the wide load/store.
3020 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override {
3021 llvm_unreachable("VPWidenMemoryRecipe should not be instantiated.");
3022 }
3023
3024 /// Return the cost of this VPWidenMemoryRecipe.
3025 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3026 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
3027
3028 Instruction &getIngredient() const { return Ingredient; }
3029};
3030
3031/// A recipe for widening load operations, using the address to load from and an
3032/// optional mask.
3033struct VPWidenLoadRecipe final : public VPWidenMemoryRecipe, public VPValue {
3034 VPWidenLoadRecipe(LoadInst &Load, VPValue *Addr, VPValue *Mask,
3035 bool Consecutive, bool Reverse, DebugLoc DL)
3036 : VPWidenMemoryRecipe(VPDef::VPWidenLoadSC, Load, {Addr}, Consecutive,
3037 Reverse, DL),
3038 VPValue(this, &Load) {
3039 setMask(Mask);
3040 }
3041
3042 VPWidenLoadRecipe *clone() override {
3043 return new VPWidenLoadRecipe(cast<LoadInst>(Ingredient), getAddr(),
3044 getMask(), Consecutive, Reverse,
3045 getDebugLoc());
3046 }
3047
3048 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPWidenLoadSC);
3049
3050 /// Generate a wide load or gather.
3051 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3052
3053#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3054 /// Print the recipe.
3055 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3056 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3057#endif
3058
3059 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
3060 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3061 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3062 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3063 // Widened, consecutive loads operations only demand the first lane of
3064 // their address.
3065 return Op == getAddr() && isConsecutive();
3066 }
3067};
3068
3069/// A recipe for widening load operations with vector-predication intrinsics,
3070/// using the address to load from, the explicit vector length and an optional
3071/// mask.
3072struct VPWidenLoadEVLRecipe final : public VPWidenMemoryRecipe, public VPValue {
3073 VPWidenLoadEVLRecipe(VPWidenLoadRecipe &L, VPValue &EVL, VPValue *Mask)
3074 : VPWidenMemoryRecipe(VPDef::VPWidenLoadEVLSC, L.getIngredient(),
3075 {L.getAddr(), &EVL}, L.isConsecutive(),
3076 L.isReverse(), L.getDebugLoc()),
3077 VPValue(this, &getIngredient()) {
3078 setMask(Mask);
3079 }
3080
3081 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPWidenLoadEVLSC)
3082
3083 /// Return the EVL operand.
3084 VPValue *getEVL() const { return getOperand(1); }
3085
3086 /// Generate the wide load or gather.
3087 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3088
3089 /// Return the cost of this VPWidenLoadEVLRecipe.
3090 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3091 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
3092
3093#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3094 /// Print the recipe.
3095 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3096 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3097#endif
3098
3099 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
3100 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3101 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3102 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3103 // Widened loads only demand the first lane of EVL and consecutive loads
3104 // only demand the first lane of their address.
3105 return Op == getEVL() || (Op == getAddr() && isConsecutive());
3106 }
3107};
3108
3109/// A recipe for widening store operations, using the stored value, the address
3110/// to store to and an optional mask.
3111struct VPWidenStoreRecipe final : public VPWidenMemoryRecipe {
3112 VPWidenStoreRecipe(StoreInst &Store, VPValue *Addr, VPValue *StoredVal,
3113 VPValue *Mask, bool Consecutive, bool Reverse, DebugLoc DL)
3114 : VPWidenMemoryRecipe(VPDef::VPWidenStoreSC, Store, {Addr, StoredVal},
3115 Consecutive, Reverse, DL) {
3116 setMask(Mask);
3117 }
3118
3119 VPWidenStoreRecipe *clone() override {
3120 return new VPWidenStoreRecipe(cast<StoreInst>(Ingredient), getAddr(),
3121 getStoredValue(), getMask(), Consecutive,
3122 Reverse, getDebugLoc());
3123 }
3124
3125 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPWidenStoreSC);
3126
3127 /// Return the value stored by this recipe.
3128 VPValue *getStoredValue() const { return getOperand(1); }
3129
3130 /// Generate a wide store or scatter.
3131 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3132
3133#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3134 /// Print the recipe.
3135 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3136 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3137#endif
3138
3139 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
3140 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3141 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3142 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3143 // Widened, consecutive stores only demand the first lane of their address,
3144 // unless the same operand is also stored.
3145 return Op == getAddr() && isConsecutive() && Op != getStoredValue();
3146 }
3147};
3148
3149/// A recipe for widening store operations with vector-predication intrinsics,
3150/// using the value to store, the address to store to, the explicit vector
3151/// length and an optional mask.
3152struct VPWidenStoreEVLRecipe final : public VPWidenMemoryRecipe {
3153 VPWidenStoreEVLRecipe(VPWidenStoreRecipe &S, VPValue &EVL, VPValue *Mask)
3154 : VPWidenMemoryRecipe(VPDef::VPWidenStoreEVLSC, S.getIngredient(),
3155 {S.getAddr(), S.getStoredValue(), &EVL},
3156 S.isConsecutive(), S.isReverse(), S.getDebugLoc()) {
3157 setMask(Mask);
3158 }
3159
3160 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPWidenStoreEVLSC)
3161
3162 /// Return the address accessed by this recipe.
3163 VPValue *getStoredValue() const { return getOperand(1); }
3164
3165 /// Return the EVL operand.
3166 VPValue *getEVL() const { return getOperand(2); }
3167
3168 /// Generate the wide store or scatter.
3169 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3170
3171 /// Return the cost of this VPWidenStoreEVLRecipe.
3172 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3173 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override;
3174
3175#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3176 /// Print the recipe.
3177 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3178 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3179#endif
3180
3181 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
3182 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3183 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3184 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3185 if (Op == getEVL()) {
3186 assert(getStoredValue() != Op && "unexpected store of EVL");
3187 return true;
3188 }
3189 // Widened, consecutive memory operations only demand the first lane of
3190 // their address, unless the same operand is also stored. That latter can
3191 // happen with opaque pointers.
3192 return Op == getAddr() && isConsecutive() && Op != getStoredValue();
3193 }
3194};
3195
3196/// Recipe to expand a SCEV expression.
3197class VPExpandSCEVRecipe : public VPSingleDefRecipe {
3198 const SCEV *Expr;
3199 ScalarEvolution &SE;
3200
3201public:
3202 VPExpandSCEVRecipe(const SCEV *Expr, ScalarEvolution &SE)
3203 : VPSingleDefRecipe(VPDef::VPExpandSCEVSC, {}), Expr(Expr), SE(SE) {}
3204
3205 ~VPExpandSCEVRecipe() override = default;
3206
3207 VPExpandSCEVRecipe *clone() override {
3208 return new VPExpandSCEVRecipe(Expr, SE);
3209 }
3210
3211 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPExpandSCEVSC)
3212
3213 /// Generate a canonical vector induction variable of the vector loop, with
3214 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3215
3216 /// Return the cost of this VPExpandSCEVRecipe.
3217 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3218 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override {
3219 // TODO: Compute accurate cost after retiring the legacy cost model.
3220 return 0;
3221 }
3222
3223#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3224 /// Print the recipe.
3225 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3226 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3227#endif
3228
3229 const SCEV *getSCEV() const { return Expr; }
3230};
3231
3232/// Canonical scalar induction phi of the vector loop. Starting at the specified
3233/// start value (either 0 or the resume value when vectorizing the epilogue
3234/// loop). VPWidenCanonicalIVRecipe represents the vector version of the
3235/// canonical induction variable.
3236class VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe : public VPHeaderPHIRecipe {
3237public:
3238 VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe(VPValue *StartV, DebugLoc DL)
3239 : VPHeaderPHIRecipe(VPDef::VPCanonicalIVPHISC, nullptr, StartV, DL) {}
3240
3241 ~VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe() override = default;
3242
3243 VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe *clone() override {
3244 auto *R = new VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe(getOperand(0), getDebugLoc());
3245 R->addOperand(getBackedgeValue());
3246 return R;
3247 }
3248
3249 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPCanonicalIVPHISC)
3250
3251 static inline bool classof(const VPHeaderPHIRecipe *D) {
3252 return D->getVPDefID() == VPDef::VPCanonicalIVPHISC;
3253 }
3254
3255 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override {
3256 llvm_unreachable(
3257 "cannot execute this recipe, should be replaced by VPScalarPHIRecipe");
3258 }
3259
3260#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3261 /// Print the recipe.
3262 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3263 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3264#endif
3265
3266 /// Returns the scalar type of the induction.
3267 Type *getScalarType() const {
3268 return getStartValue()->getLiveInIRValue()->getType();
3269 }
3270
3271 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
3272 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3273 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3274 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3275 return true;
3276 }
3277
3278 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first part of operand \p Op.
3279 bool onlyFirstPartUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3280 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3281 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3282 return true;
3283 }
3284
3285 /// Return the cost of this VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe.
3286 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3287 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override {
3288 // For now, match the behavior of the legacy cost model.
3289 return 0;
3290 }
3291};
3292
3293/// A recipe for generating the active lane mask for the vector loop that is
3294/// used to predicate the vector operations.
3295/// TODO: It would be good to use the existing VPWidenPHIRecipe instead and
3296/// remove VPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipe.
3297class VPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipe : public VPHeaderPHIRecipe {
3298public:
3299 VPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipe(VPValue *StartMask, DebugLoc DL)
3300 : VPHeaderPHIRecipe(VPDef::VPActiveLaneMaskPHISC, nullptr, StartMask,
3301 DL) {}
3302
3303 ~VPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipe() override = default;
3304
3305 VPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipe *clone() override {
3306 auto *R = new VPActiveLaneMaskPHIRecipe(getOperand(0), getDebugLoc());
3307 if (getNumOperands() == 2)
3308 R->addOperand(getOperand(1));
3309 return R;
3310 }
3311
3312 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPActiveLaneMaskPHISC)
3313
3314 static inline bool classof(const VPHeaderPHIRecipe *D) {
3315 return D->getVPDefID() == VPDef::VPActiveLaneMaskPHISC;
3316 }
3317
3318 /// Generate the active lane mask phi of the vector loop.
3319 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3320
3321#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3322 /// Print the recipe.
3323 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3324 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3325#endif
3326};
3327
3328/// A recipe for generating the phi node for the current index of elements,
3329/// adjusted in accordance with EVL value. It starts at the start value of the
3330/// canonical induction and gets incremented by EVL in each iteration of the
3331/// vector loop.
3332class VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe : public VPHeaderPHIRecipe {
3333public:
3334 VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe(VPValue *StartIV, DebugLoc DL)
3335 : VPHeaderPHIRecipe(VPDef::VPEVLBasedIVPHISC, nullptr, StartIV, DL) {}
3336
3337 ~VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe() override = default;
3338
3339 VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe *clone() override {
3340 llvm_unreachable("cloning not implemented yet");
3341 }
3342
3343 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPEVLBasedIVPHISC)
3344
3345 static inline bool classof(const VPHeaderPHIRecipe *D) {
3346 return D->getVPDefID() == VPDef::VPEVLBasedIVPHISC;
3347 }
3348
3349 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override {
3350 llvm_unreachable(
3351 "cannot execute this recipe, should be replaced by VPScalarPHIRecipe");
3352 }
3353
3354 /// Return the cost of this VPEVLBasedIVPHIRecipe.
3355 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3356 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override {
3357 // For now, match the behavior of the legacy cost model.
3358 return 0;
3359 }
3360
3361 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
3362 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3363 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3364 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3365 return true;
3366 }
3367
3368#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3369 /// Print the recipe.
3370 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3371 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3372#endif
3373};
3374
3375/// A Recipe for widening the canonical induction variable of the vector loop.
3376class VPWidenCanonicalIVRecipe : public VPSingleDefRecipe,
3377 public VPUnrollPartAccessor<1> {
3378public:
3379 VPWidenCanonicalIVRecipe(VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe *CanonicalIV)
3380 : VPSingleDefRecipe(VPDef::VPWidenCanonicalIVSC, {CanonicalIV}) {}
3381
3382 ~VPWidenCanonicalIVRecipe() override = default;
3383
3384 VPWidenCanonicalIVRecipe *clone() override {
3385 return new VPWidenCanonicalIVRecipe(
3386 cast<VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe>(getOperand(0)));
3387 }
3388
3389 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPWidenCanonicalIVSC)
3390
3391 /// Generate a canonical vector induction variable of the vector loop, with
3392 /// start = {<Part*VF, Part*VF+1, ..., Part*VF+VF-1> for 0 <= Part < UF}, and
3393 /// step = <VF*UF, VF*UF, ..., VF*UF>.
3394 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3395
3396 /// Return the cost of this VPWidenCanonicalIVPHIRecipe.
3397 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3398 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override {
3399 // TODO: Compute accurate cost after retiring the legacy cost model.
3400 return 0;
3401 }
3402
3403#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3404 /// Print the recipe.
3405 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3406 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3407#endif
3408};
3409
3410/// A recipe for converting the input value \p IV value to the corresponding
3411/// value of an IV with different start and step values, using Start + IV *
3412/// Step.
3413class VPDerivedIVRecipe : public VPSingleDefRecipe {
3414 /// Kind of the induction.
3415 const InductionDescriptor::InductionKind Kind;
3416 /// If not nullptr, the floating point induction binary operator. Must be set
3417 /// for floating point inductions.
3418 const FPMathOperator *FPBinOp;
3419
3420 /// Name to use for the generated IR instruction for the derived IV.
3421 std::string Name;
3422
3423public:
3424 VPDerivedIVRecipe(const InductionDescriptor &IndDesc, VPValue *Start,
3425 VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe *CanonicalIV, VPValue *Step,
3426 const Twine &Name = "")
3427 : VPDerivedIVRecipe(
3428 IndDesc.getKind(),
3429 dyn_cast_or_null<FPMathOperator>(IndDesc.getInductionBinOp()),
3430 Start, CanonicalIV, Step, Name) {}
3431
3432 VPDerivedIVRecipe(InductionDescriptor::InductionKind Kind,
3433 const FPMathOperator *FPBinOp, VPValue *Start, VPValue *IV,
3434 VPValue *Step, const Twine &Name = "")
3435 : VPSingleDefRecipe(VPDef::VPDerivedIVSC, {Start, IV, Step}), Kind(Kind),
3436 FPBinOp(FPBinOp), Name(Name.str()) {}
3437
3438 ~VPDerivedIVRecipe() override = default;
3439
3440 VPDerivedIVRecipe *clone() override {
3441 return new VPDerivedIVRecipe(Kind, FPBinOp, getStartValue(), getOperand(1),
3442 getStepValue());
3443 }
3444
3445 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPDerivedIVSC)
3446
3447 /// Generate the transformed value of the induction at offset StartValue (1.
3448 /// operand) + IV (2. operand) * StepValue (3, operand).
3449 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3450
3451 /// Return the cost of this VPDerivedIVRecipe.
3452 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3453 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override {
3454 // TODO: Compute accurate cost after retiring the legacy cost model.
3455 return 0;
3456 }
3457
3458#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3459 /// Print the recipe.
3460 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3461 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3462#endif
3463
3464 Type *getScalarType() const {
3465 return getStartValue()->getLiveInIRValue()->getType();
3466 }
3467
3468 VPValue *getStartValue() const { return getOperand(0); }
3469 VPValue *getStepValue() const { return getOperand(2); }
3470
3471 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
3472 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3473 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3474 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3475 return true;
3476 }
3477};
3478
3479/// A recipe for handling phi nodes of integer and floating-point inductions,
3480/// producing their scalar values.
3481class VPScalarIVStepsRecipe : public VPRecipeWithIRFlags,
3482 public VPUnrollPartAccessor<2> {
3483 Instruction::BinaryOps InductionOpcode;
3484
3485public:
3486 VPScalarIVStepsRecipe(VPValue *IV, VPValue *Step,
3487 Instruction::BinaryOps Opcode, FastMathFlags FMFs)
3488 : VPRecipeWithIRFlags(VPDef::VPScalarIVStepsSC,
3489 ArrayRef<VPValue *>({IV, Step}), FMFs),
3490 InductionOpcode(Opcode) {}
3491
3492 VPScalarIVStepsRecipe(const InductionDescriptor &IndDesc, VPValue *IV,
3493 VPValue *Step)
3494 : VPScalarIVStepsRecipe(
3495 IV, Step, IndDesc.getInductionOpcode(),
3496 dyn_cast_or_null<FPMathOperator>(IndDesc.getInductionBinOp())
3497 ? IndDesc.getInductionBinOp()->getFastMathFlags()
3498 : FastMathFlags()) {}
3499
3500 ~VPScalarIVStepsRecipe() override = default;
3501
3502 VPScalarIVStepsRecipe *clone() override {
3503 return new VPScalarIVStepsRecipe(
3504 getOperand(0), getOperand(1), InductionOpcode,
3505 hasFastMathFlags() ? getFastMathFlags() : FastMathFlags());
3506 }
3507
3508 VP_CLASSOF_IMPL(VPDef::VPScalarIVStepsSC)
3509
3510 /// Generate the scalarized versions of the phi node as needed by their users.
3511 void execute(VPTransformState &State) override;
3512
3513 /// Return the cost of this VPScalarIVStepsRecipe.
3514 InstructionCost computeCost(ElementCount VF,
3515 VPCostContext &Ctx) const override {
3516 // TODO: Compute accurate cost after retiring the legacy cost model.
3517 return 0;
3518 }
3519
3520#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3521 /// Print the recipe.
3522 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3523 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3524#endif
3525
3526 VPValue *getStepValue() const { return getOperand(1); }
3527
3528 /// Returns true if the recipe only uses the first lane of operand \p Op.
3529 bool onlyFirstLaneUsed(const VPValue *Op) const override {
3530 assert(is_contained(operands(), Op) &&
3531 "Op must be an operand of the recipe");
3532 return true;
3533 }
3534};
3535
3536/// VPBasicBlock serves as the leaf of the Hierarchical Control-Flow Graph. It
3537/// holds a sequence of zero or more VPRecipe's each representing a sequence of
3538/// output IR instructions. All PHI-like recipes must come before any non-PHI recipes.
3539class VPBasicBlock : public VPBlockBase {
3540 friend class VPlan;
3541
3542 /// Use VPlan::createVPBasicBlock to create VPBasicBlocks.
3543 VPBasicBlock(const Twine &Name = "", VPRecipeBase *Recipe = nullptr)
3544 : VPBlockBase(VPBasicBlockSC, Name.str()) {
3545 if (Recipe)
3546 appendRecipe(Recipe);
3547 }
3548
3549public:
3550 using RecipeListTy = iplist<VPRecipeBase>;
3551
3552protected:
3553 /// The VPRecipes held in the order of output instructions to generate.
3554 RecipeListTy Recipes;
3555
3556 VPBasicBlock(const unsigned char BlockSC, const Twine &Name = "")
3557 : VPBlockBase(BlockSC, Name.str()) {}
3558
3559public:
3560 ~VPBasicBlock() override {
3561 while (!Recipes.empty())
3562 Recipes.pop_back();
3563 }
3564
3565 /// Instruction iterators...
3566 using iterator = RecipeListTy::iterator;
3567 using const_iterator = RecipeListTy::const_iterator;
3568 using reverse_iterator = RecipeListTy::reverse_iterator;
3569 using const_reverse_iterator = RecipeListTy::const_reverse_iterator;
3570
3571 //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
3572 /// Recipe iterator methods
3573 ///
3574 inline iterator begin() { return Recipes.begin(); }
3575 inline const_iterator begin() const { return Recipes.begin(); }
3576 inline iterator end() { return Recipes.end(); }
3577 inline const_iterator end() const { return Recipes.end(); }
3578
3579 inline reverse_iterator rbegin() { return Recipes.rbegin(); }
3580 inline const_reverse_iterator rbegin() const { return Recipes.rbegin(); }
3581 inline reverse_iterator rend() { return Recipes.rend(); }
3582 inline const_reverse_iterator rend() const { return Recipes.rend(); }
3583
3584 inline size_t size() const { return Recipes.size(); }
3585 inline bool empty() const { return Recipes.empty(); }
3586 inline const VPRecipeBase &front() const { return Recipes.front(); }
3587 inline VPRecipeBase &front() { return Recipes.front(); }
3588 inline const VPRecipeBase &back() const { return Recipes.back(); }
3589 inline VPRecipeBase &back() { return Recipes.back(); }
3590
3591 /// Returns a reference to the list of recipes.
3592 RecipeListTy &getRecipeList() { return Recipes; }
3593
3594 /// Returns a pointer to a member of the recipe list.
3595 static RecipeListTy VPBasicBlock::*getSublistAccess(VPRecipeBase *) {
3596 return &VPBasicBlock::Recipes;
3597 }
3598
3599 /// Method to support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.
3600 static inline bool classof(const VPBlockBase *V) {
3601 return V->getVPBlockID() == VPBlockBase::VPBasicBlockSC ||
3602 V->getVPBlockID() == VPBlockBase::VPIRBasicBlockSC;
3603 }
3604
3605 void insert(VPRecipeBase *Recipe, iterator InsertPt) {
3606 assert(Recipe && "No recipe to append.");
3607 assert(!Recipe->Parent && "Recipe already in VPlan");
3608 Recipe->Parent = this;
3609 Recipes.insert(InsertPt, Recipe);
3610 }
3611
3612 /// Augment the existing recipes of a VPBasicBlock with an additional
3613 /// \p Recipe as the last recipe.
3614 void appendRecipe(VPRecipeBase *Recipe) { insert(Recipe, end()); }
3615
3616 /// The method which generates the output IR instructions that correspond to
3617 /// this VPBasicBlock, thereby "executing" the VPlan.
3618 void execute(VPTransformState *State) override;
3619
3620 /// Return the cost of this VPBasicBlock.
3621 InstructionCost cost(ElementCount VF, VPCostContext &Ctx) override;
3622
3623 /// Return the position of the first non-phi node recipe in the block.
3624 iterator getFirstNonPhi();
3625
3626 /// Returns an iterator range over the PHI-like recipes in the block.
3627 iterator_range<iterator> phis() {
3628 return make_range(begin(), getFirstNonPhi());
3629 }
3630
3631 /// Split current block at \p SplitAt by inserting a new block between the
3632 /// current block and its successors and moving all recipes starting at
3633 /// SplitAt to the new block. Returns the new block.
3634 VPBasicBlock *splitAt(iterator SplitAt);
3635
3636 VPRegionBlock *getEnclosingLoopRegion();
3637 const VPRegionBlock *getEnclosingLoopRegion() const;
3638
3639#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3640 /// Print this VPBsicBlock to \p O, prefixing all lines with \p Indent. \p
3641 /// SlotTracker is used to print unnamed VPValue's using consequtive numbers.
3642 ///
3643 /// Note that the numbering is applied to the whole VPlan, so printing
3644 /// individual blocks is consistent with the whole VPlan printing.
3645 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3646 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3647 using VPBlockBase::print; // Get the print(raw_stream &O) version.
3648#endif
3649
3650 /// If the block has multiple successors, return the branch recipe terminating
3651 /// the block. If there are no or only a single successor, return nullptr;
3652 VPRecipeBase *getTerminator();
3653 const VPRecipeBase *getTerminator() const;
3654
3655 /// Returns true if the block is exiting it's parent region.
3656 bool isExiting() const;
3657
3658 /// Clone the current block and it's recipes, without updating the operands of
3659 /// the cloned recipes.
3660 VPBasicBlock *clone() override;
3661
3662protected:
3663 /// Execute the recipes in the IR basic block \p BB.
3664 void executeRecipes(VPTransformState *State, BasicBlock *BB);
3665
3666 /// Connect the VPBBs predecessors' in the VPlan CFG to the IR basic block
3667 /// generated for this VPBB.
3668 void connectToPredecessors(VPTransformState::CFGState &CFG);
3669
3670private:
3671 /// Create an IR BasicBlock to hold the output instructions generated by this
3672 /// VPBasicBlock, and return it. Update the CFGState accordingly.
3673 BasicBlock *createEmptyBasicBlock(VPTransformState::CFGState &CFG);
3674};
3675
3676/// A special type of VPBasicBlock that wraps an existing IR basic block.
3677/// Recipes of the block get added before the first non-phi instruction in the
3678/// wrapped block.
3679/// Note: At the moment, VPIRBasicBlock can only be used to wrap VPlan's
3680/// preheader block.
3681class VPIRBasicBlock : public VPBasicBlock {
3682 friend class VPlan;
3683
3684 BasicBlock *IRBB;
3685
3686 /// Use VPlan::createVPIRBasicBlock to create VPIRBasicBlocks.
3687 VPIRBasicBlock(BasicBlock *IRBB)
3688 : VPBasicBlock(VPIRBasicBlockSC,
3689 (Twine("ir-bb<") + IRBB->getName() + Twine(">")).str()),
3690 IRBB(IRBB) {}
3691
3692public:
3693 ~VPIRBasicBlock() override {}
3694
3695 static inline bool classof(const VPBlockBase *V) {
3696 return V->getVPBlockID() == VPBlockBase::VPIRBasicBlockSC;
3697 }
3698
3699 /// The method which generates the output IR instructions that correspond to
3700 /// this VPBasicBlock, thereby "executing" the VPlan.
3701 void execute(VPTransformState *State) override;
3702
3703 VPIRBasicBlock *clone() override;
3704
3705 BasicBlock *getIRBasicBlock() const { return IRBB; }
3706};
3707
3708/// VPRegionBlock represents a collection of VPBasicBlocks and VPRegionBlocks
3709/// which form a Single-Entry-Single-Exiting subgraph of the output IR CFG.
3710/// A VPRegionBlock may indicate that its contents are to be replicated several
3711/// times. This is designed to support predicated scalarization, in which a
3712/// scalar if-then code structure needs to be generated VF * UF times. Having
3713/// this replication indicator helps to keep a single model for multiple
3714/// candidate VF's. The actual replication takes place only once the desired VF
3715/// and UF have been determined.
3716class VPRegionBlock : public VPBlockBase {
3717 friend class VPlan;
3718
3719 /// Hold the Single Entry of the SESE region modelled by the VPRegionBlock.
3720 VPBlockBase *Entry;
3721
3722 /// Hold the Single Exiting block of the SESE region modelled by the
3723 /// VPRegionBlock.
3724 VPBlockBase *Exiting;
3725
3726 /// An indicator whether this region is to generate multiple replicated
3727 /// instances of output IR corresponding to its VPBlockBases.
3728 bool IsReplicator;
3729
3730 /// Use VPlan::createVPRegionBlock to create VPRegionBlocks.
3731 VPRegionBlock(VPBlockBase *Entry, VPBlockBase *Exiting,
3732 const std::string &Name = "", bool IsReplicator = false)
3733 : VPBlockBase(VPRegionBlockSC, Name), Entry(Entry), Exiting(Exiting),
3734 IsReplicator(IsReplicator) {
3735 assert(Entry->getPredecessors().empty() && "Entry block has predecessors.");
3736 assert(Exiting->getSuccessors().empty() && "Exit block has successors.");
3737 Entry->setParent(this);
3738 Exiting->setParent(this);
3739 }
3740 VPRegionBlock(const std::string &Name = "", bool IsReplicator = false)
3741 : VPBlockBase(VPRegionBlockSC, Name), Entry(nullptr), Exiting(nullptr),
3742 IsReplicator(IsReplicator) {}
3743
3744public:
3745 ~VPRegionBlock() override {}
3746
3747 /// Method to support type inquiry through isa, cast, and dyn_cast.
3748 static inline bool classof(const VPBlockBase *V) {
3749 return V->getVPBlockID() == VPBlockBase::VPRegionBlockSC;
3750 }
3751
3752 const VPBlockBase *getEntry() const { return Entry; }
3753 VPBlockBase *getEntry() { return Entry; }
3754
3755 /// Set \p EntryBlock as the entry VPBlockBase of this VPRegionBlock. \p
3756 /// EntryBlock must have no predecessors.
3757 void setEntry(VPBlockBase *EntryBlock) {
3758 assert(EntryBlock->getPredecessors().empty() &&
3759 "Entry block cannot have predecessors.");
3760 Entry = EntryBlock;
3761 EntryBlock->setParent(this);
3762 }
3763
3764 const VPBlockBase *getExiting() const { return Exiting; }
3765 VPBlockBase *getExiting() { return Exiting; }
3766
3767 /// Set \p ExitingBlock as the exiting VPBlockBase of this VPRegionBlock. \p
3768 /// ExitingBlock must have no successors.
3769 void setExiting(VPBlockBase *ExitingBlock) {
3770 assert(ExitingBlock->getSuccessors().empty() &&
3771 "Exit block cannot have successors.");
3772 Exiting = ExitingBlock;
3773 ExitingBlock->setParent(this);
3774 }
3775
3776 /// Returns the pre-header VPBasicBlock of the loop region.
3777 VPBasicBlock *getPreheaderVPBB() {
3778 assert(!isReplicator() && "should only get pre-header of loop regions");
3779 return getSinglePredecessor()->getExitingBasicBlock();
3780 }
3781
3782 /// An indicator whether this region is to generate multiple replicated
3783 /// instances of output IR corresponding to its VPBlockBases.
3784 bool isReplicator() const { return IsReplicator; }
3785
3786 /// The method which generates the output IR instructions that correspond to
3787 /// this VPRegionBlock, thereby "executing" the VPlan.
3788 void execute(VPTransformState *State) override;
3789
3790 // Return the cost of this region.
3791 InstructionCost cost(ElementCount VF, VPCostContext &Ctx) override;
3792
3793#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
3794 /// Print this VPRegionBlock to \p O (recursively), prefixing all lines with
3795 /// \p Indent. \p SlotTracker is used to print unnamed VPValue's using
3796 /// consequtive numbers.
3797 ///
3798 /// Note that the numbering is applied to the whole VPlan, so printing
3799 /// individual regions is consistent with the whole VPlan printing.
3800 void print(raw_ostream &O, const Twine &Indent,
3801 VPSlotTracker &SlotTracker) const override;
3802 using VPBlockBase::print; // Get the print(raw_stream &O) version.
3803#endif
3804
3805 /// Clone all blocks in the single-entry single-exit region of the block and
3806 /// their recipes without updating the operands of the cloned recipes.
3807 VPRegionBlock *clone() override;
3808};
3809
3810/// VPlan models a candidate for vectorization, encoding various decisions take
3811/// to produce efficient output IR, including which branches, basic-blocks and
3812/// output IR instructions to generate, and their cost. VPlan holds a
3813/// Hierarchical-CFG of VPBasicBlocks and VPRegionBlocks rooted at an Entry
3814/// VPBasicBlock.
3815class VPlan {
3816 friend class VPlanPrinter;
3817 friend class VPSlotTracker;
3818
3819 /// VPBasicBlock corresponding to the original preheader. Used to place
3820 /// VPExpandSCEV recipes for expressions used during skeleton creation and the
3821 /// rest of VPlan execution.
3822 /// When this VPlan is used for the epilogue vector loop, the entry will be
3823 /// replaced by a new entry block created during skeleton creation.
3824 VPBasicBlock *Entry;
3825
3826 /// VPIRBasicBlock wrapping the header of the original scalar loop.
3827 VPIRBasicBlock *ScalarHeader;
3828
3829 /// Holds the VFs applicable to this VPlan.
3830 SmallSetVector<ElementCount, 2> VFs;
3831
3832 /// Holds the UFs applicable to this VPlan. If empty, the VPlan is valid for
3833 /// any UF.
3834 SmallSetVector<unsigned, 2> UFs;
3835
3836 /// Holds the name of the VPlan, for printing.
3837 std::string Name;
3838
3839 /// Represents the trip count of the original loop, for folding
3840 /// the tail.
3841 VPValue *TripCount = nullptr;
3842
3843 /// Represents the backedge taken count of the original loop, for folding
3844 /// the tail. It equals TripCount - 1.
3845 VPValue *BackedgeTakenCount = nullptr;
3846
3847 /// Represents the vector trip count.
3848 VPValue VectorTripCount;
3849
3850 /// Represents the vectorization factor of the loop.
3851 VPValue VF;
3852
3853 /// Represents the loop-invariant VF * UF of the vector loop region.
3854 VPValue VFxUF;
3855
3856 /// Holds a mapping between Values and their corresponding VPValue inside
3857 /// VPlan.
3858 Value2VPValueTy Value2VPValue;
3859
3860 /// Contains all the external definitions created for this VPlan. External
3861 /// definitions are VPValues that hold a pointer to their underlying IR.
3862 SmallVector<VPValue *, 16> VPLiveInsToFree;
3863
3864 /// Mapping from SCEVs to the VPValues representing their expansions.
3865 /// NOTE: This mapping is temporary and will be removed once all users have
3866 /// been modeled in VPlan directly.
3867 DenseMap<const SCEV *, VPValue *> SCEVToExpansion;
3868
3869 /// Blocks allocated and owned by the VPlan. They will be deleted once the
3870 /// VPlan is destroyed.
3871 SmallVector<VPBlockBase *> CreatedBlocks;
3872
3873 /// Construct a VPlan with \p Entry to the plan and with \p ScalarHeader
3874 /// wrapping the original header of the scalar loop.
3875 VPlan(VPBasicBlock *Entry, VPIRBasicBlock *ScalarHeader)
3876 : Entry(Entry), ScalarHeader(ScalarHeader) {
3877 Entry->setPlan(this);
3878 assert(ScalarHeader->getNumSuccessors() == 0 &&
3879 "scalar header must be a leaf node");
3880 }
3881
3882public:
3883 /// Construct a VPlan for \p L. This will create VPIRBasicBlocks wrapping the
3884 /// original preheader and scalar header of \p L, to be used as entry and
3885 /// scalar header blocks of the new VPlan.
3886 VPlan(Loop *L);
3887
3888 /// Construct a VPlan with a new VPBasicBlock as entry, a VPIRBasicBlock
3889 /// wrapping \p ScalarHeaderBB and a trip count of \p TC.
3890 VPlan(BasicBlock *ScalarHeaderBB, VPValue *TC) {
3891 setEntry(createVPBasicBlock("preheader"));
3892 ScalarHeader = createVPIRBasicBlock(ScalarHeaderBB);
3893 TripCount = TC;
3894 }
3895
3896 ~VPlan();
3897
3898 void setEntry(VPBasicBlock *VPBB) {
3899 Entry = VPBB;
3900 VPBB->setPlan(this);
3901 }
3902
3903 /// Create initial VPlan, having an "entry" VPBasicBlock (wrapping
3904 /// original scalar pre-header) which contains SCEV expansions that need
3905 /// to happen before the CFG is modified (when executing a VPlan for the
3906 /// epilogue vector loop, the original entry needs to be replaced by a new
3907 /// one); a VPBasicBlock for the vector pre-header, followed by a region for
3908 /// the vector loop, followed by the middle VPBasicBlock. If a check is needed
3909 /// to guard executing the scalar epilogue loop, it will be added to the
3910 /// middle block, together with VPBasicBlocks for the scalar preheader and
3911 /// exit blocks. \p InductionTy is the type of the canonical induction and
3912 /// used for related values, like the trip count expression.
3913 static VPlanPtr createInitialVPlan(Type *InductionTy,
3914 PredicatedScalarEvolution &PSE,
3915 bool RequiresScalarEpilogueCheck,
3916 bool TailFolded, Loop *TheLoop);
3917
3918 /// Prepare the plan for execution, setting up the required live-in values.
3919 void prepareToExecute(Value *TripCount, Value *VectorTripCount,
3920 VPTransformState &State);
3921
3922 /// Generate the IR code for this VPlan.
3923 void execute(VPTransformState *State);
3924
3925 /// Return the cost of this plan.
3926 InstructionCost cost(ElementCount VF, VPCostContext &Ctx);
3927
3928 VPBasicBlock *getEntry() { return Entry; }
3929 const VPBasicBlock *getEntry() const { return Entry; }
3930
3931 /// Returns the preheader of the vector loop region, if one exists, or null
3932 /// otherwise.
3933 VPBasicBlock *getVectorPreheader() {
3934 VPRegionBlock *VectorRegion = getVectorLoopRegion();
3935 return VectorRegion
3936 ? cast<VPBasicBlock>(VectorRegion->getSinglePredecessor())
3937 : nullptr;
3938 }
3939
3940 /// Returns the VPRegionBlock of the vector loop.
3941 VPRegionBlock *getVectorLoopRegion();
3942 const VPRegionBlock *getVectorLoopRegion() const;
3943
3944 /// Returns the 'middle' block of the plan, that is the block that selects
3945 /// whether to execute the scalar tail loop or the exit block from the loop
3946 /// latch.
3947 const VPBasicBlock *getMiddleBlock() const {
3948 return cast<VPBasicBlock>(getScalarPreheader()->getPredecessors().front());
3949 }
3950 VPBasicBlock *getMiddleBlock() {
3951 return cast<VPBasicBlock>(getScalarPreheader()->getPredecessors().front());
3952 }
3953
3954 /// Return the VPBasicBlock for the preheader of the scalar loop.
3955 VPBasicBlock *getScalarPreheader() const {
3956 return cast<VPBasicBlock>(getScalarHeader()->getSinglePredecessor());
3957 }
3958
3959 /// Return the VPIRBasicBlock wrapping the header of the scalar loop.
3960 VPIRBasicBlock *getScalarHeader() const { return ScalarHeader; }
3961
3962 /// Return an iterator range over the VPIRBasicBlock wrapping the exit blocks
3963 /// of the VPlan, that is leaf nodes except the scalar header. Defined in
3964 /// VPlanHCFG, as the definition of the type needs access to the definitions
3965 /// of VPBlockShallowTraversalWrapper.
3966 auto getExitBlocks();
3967
3968 /// The trip count of the original loop.
3969 VPValue *getTripCount() const {
3970 assert(TripCount && "trip count needs to be set before accessing it");
3971 return TripCount;
3972 }
3973
3974 /// Resets the trip count for the VPlan. The caller must make sure all uses of
3975 /// the original trip count have been replaced.
3976 void resetTripCount(VPValue *NewTripCount) {
3977 assert(TripCount && NewTripCount && TripCount->getNumUsers() == 0 &&
3978 "TripCount always must be set");
3979 TripCount = NewTripCount;
3980 }
3981
3982 /// The backedge taken count of the original loop.
3983 VPValue *getOrCreateBackedgeTakenCount() {
3984 if (!BackedgeTakenCount)
3985 BackedgeTakenCount = new VPValue();
3986 return BackedgeTakenCount;
3987 }
3988
3989 /// The vector trip count.
3990 VPValue &getVectorTripCount() { return VectorTripCount; }
3991
3992 /// Returns the VF of the vector loop region.
3993 VPValue &getVF() { return VF; };
3994
3995 /// Returns VF * UF of the vector loop region.
3996 VPValue &getVFxUF() { return VFxUF; }
3997
3998 void addVF(ElementCount VF) { VFs.insert(VF); }
3999
4000 void setVF(ElementCount VF) {
4001 assert(hasVF(VF) && "Cannot set VF not already in plan");
4002 VFs.clear();
4003 VFs.insert(VF);
4004 }
4005
4006 bool hasVF(ElementCount VF) { return VFs.count(VF); }
4007 bool hasScalableVF() {
4008 return any_of(VFs, [](ElementCount VF) { return VF.isScalable(); });
4009 }
4010
4011 /// Returns an iterator range over all VFs of the plan.
4012 iterator_range<SmallSetVector<ElementCount, 2>::iterator>
4013 vectorFactors() const {
4014 return {VFs.begin(), VFs.end()};
4015 }
4016
4017 bool hasScalarVFOnly() const { return VFs.size() == 1 && VFs[0].isScalar(); }
4018
4019 bool hasUF(unsigned UF) const { return UFs.empty() || UFs.contains(UF); }
4020
4021 unsigned getUF() const {
4022 assert(UFs.size() == 1 && "Expected a single UF");
4023 return UFs[0];
4024 }
4025
4026 void setUF(unsigned UF) {
4027 assert(hasUF(UF) && "Cannot set the UF not already in plan");
4028 UFs.clear();
4029 UFs.insert(UF);
4030 }
4031
4032 /// Return a string with the name of the plan and the applicable VFs and UFs.
4033 std::string getName() const;
4034
4035 void setName(const Twine &newName) { Name = newName.str(); }
4036
4037 /// Gets the live-in VPValue for \p V or adds a new live-in (if none exists
4038 /// yet) for \p V.
4039 VPValue *getOrAddLiveIn(Value *V) {
4040 assert(V && "Trying to get or add the VPValue of a null Value");
4041 if (!Value2VPValue.count(V)) {
4042 VPValue *VPV = new VPValue(V);
4043 VPLiveInsToFree.push_back(VPV);
4044 assert(VPV->isLiveIn() && "VPV must be a live-in.");
4045 assert(!Value2VPValue.count(V) && "Value already exists in VPlan");
4046 Value2VPValue[V] = VPV;
4047 }
4048
4049 assert(Value2VPValue.count(V) && "Value does not exist in VPlan");
4050 assert(Value2VPValue[V]->isLiveIn() &&
4051 "Only live-ins should be in mapping");
4052 return Value2VPValue[V];
4053 }
4054
4055 /// Return the live-in VPValue for \p V, if there is one or nullptr otherwise.
4056 VPValue *getLiveIn(Value *V) const { return Value2VPValue.lookup(V); }
4057
4058#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
4059 /// Print the live-ins of this VPlan to \p O.
4060 void printLiveIns(raw_ostream &O) const;
4061
4062 /// Print this VPlan to \p O.
4063 void print(raw_ostream &O) const;
4064
4065 /// Print this VPlan in DOT format to \p O.
4066 void printDOT(raw_ostream &O) const;
4067
4068 /// Dump the plan to stderr (for debugging).
4069 LLVM_DUMP_METHOD void dump() const;
4070#endif
4071
4072 /// Returns the canonical induction recipe of the vector loop.
4073 VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe *getCanonicalIV() {
4074 VPBasicBlock *EntryVPBB = getVectorLoopRegion()->getEntryBasicBlock();
4075 if (EntryVPBB->empty()) {
4076 // VPlan native path.
4077 EntryVPBB = cast<VPBasicBlock>(EntryVPBB->getSingleSuccessor());
4078 }
4079 return cast<VPCanonicalIVPHIRecipe>(&*EntryVPBB->begin());
4080 }
4081
4082 VPValue *getSCEVExpansion(const SCEV *S) const {
4083 return SCEVToExpansion.lookup(S);
4084 }
4085
4086 void addSCEVExpansion(const SCEV *S, VPValue *V) {
4087 assert(!SCEVToExpansion.contains(S) && "SCEV already expanded");
4088 SCEVToExpansion[S] = V;
4089 }
4090
4091 /// Clone the current VPlan, update all VPValues of the new VPlan and cloned
4092 /// recipes to refer to the clones, and return it.
4093 VPlan *duplicate();
4094
4095 /// Create a new VPBasicBlock with \p Name and containing \p Recipe if
4096 /// present. The returned block is owned by the VPlan and deleted once the
4097 /// VPlan is destroyed.
4098 VPBasicBlock *createVPBasicBlock(const Twine &Name,
4099 VPRecipeBase *Recipe = nullptr) {
4100 auto *VPB = new VPBasicBlock(Name, Recipe);
4101 CreatedBlocks.push_back(VPB);
4102 return VPB;
4103 }
4104
4105 /// Create a new VPRegionBlock with \p Entry, \p Exiting and \p Name. If \p
4106 /// IsReplicator is true, the region is a replicate region. The returned block
4107 /// is owned by the VPlan and deleted once the VPlan is destroyed.
4108 VPRegionBlock *createVPRegionBlock(VPBlockBase *Entry, VPBlockBase *Exiting,
4109 const std::string &Name = "",
4110 bool IsReplicator = false) {
4111 auto *VPB = new VPRegionBlock(Entry, Exiting, Name, IsReplicator);
4112 CreatedBlocks.push_back(VPB);
4113 return VPB;
4114 }
4115
4116 /// Create a new VPRegionBlock with \p Name and entry and exiting blocks set
4117 /// to nullptr. If \p IsReplicator is true, the region is a replicate region.
4118 /// The returned block is owned by the VPlan and deleted once the VPlan is
4119 /// destroyed.
4120 VPRegionBlock *createVPRegionBlock(const std::string &Name = "",
4121 bool IsReplicator = false) {
4122 auto *VPB = new VPRegionBlock(Name, IsReplicator);
4123 CreatedBlocks.push_back(VPB);
4124 return VPB;
4125 }
4126
4127 /// Create a VPIRBasicBlock wrapping \p IRBB, but do not create
4128 /// VPIRInstructions wrapping the instructions in t\p IRBB. The returned
4129 /// block is owned by the VPlan and deleted once the VPlan is destroyed.
4130 VPIRBasicBlock *createEmptyVPIRBasicBlock(BasicBlock *IRBB);
4131
4132 /// Create a VPIRBasicBlock from \p IRBB containing VPIRInstructions for all
4133 /// instructions in \p IRBB, except its terminator which is managed by the
4134 /// successors of the block in VPlan. The returned block is owned by the VPlan
4135 /// and deleted once the VPlan is destroyed.
4136 VPIRBasicBlock *createVPIRBasicBlock(BasicBlock *IRBB);
4137};
4138
4139#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
4140/// VPlanPrinter prints a given VPlan to a given output stream. The printing is
4141/// indented and follows the dot format.
4142class VPlanPrinter {
4143 raw_ostream &OS;
4144 const VPlan &Plan;
4145 unsigned Depth = 0;
4146 unsigned TabWidth = 2;
4147 std::string Indent;
4148 unsigned BID = 0;
4149 SmallDenseMap<const VPBlockBase *, unsigned> BlockID;
4150
4151 VPSlotTracker SlotTracker;
4152
4153 /// Handle indentation.
4154 void bumpIndent(int b) { Indent = std::string((Depth += b) * TabWidth, ' '); }
4155
4156 /// Print a given \p Block of the Plan.
4157 void dumpBlock(const VPBlockBase *Block);
4158
4159 /// Print the information related to the CFG edges going out of a given
4160 /// \p Block, followed by printing the successor blocks themselves.
4161 void dumpEdges(const VPBlockBase *Block);
4162
4163 /// Print a given \p BasicBlock, including its VPRecipes, followed by printing
4164 /// its successor blocks.
4165 void dumpBasicBlock(const VPBasicBlock *BasicBlock);
4166
4167 /// Print a given \p Region of the Plan.
4168 void dumpRegion(const VPRegionBlock *Region);
4169
4170 unsigned getOrCreateBID(const VPBlockBase *Block) {
4171 return BlockID.count(Block) ? BlockID[Block] : BlockID[Block] = BID++;
4172 }
4173
4174 Twine getOrCreateName(const VPBlockBase *Block);
4175
4176 Twine getUID(const VPBlockBase *Block);
4177
4178 /// Print the information related to a CFG edge between two VPBlockBases.
4179 void drawEdge(const VPBlockBase *From, const VPBlockBase *To, bool Hidden,
4180 const Twine &Label);
4181
4182public:
4183 VPlanPrinter(raw_ostream &O, const VPlan &P)
4184 : OS(O), Plan(P), SlotTracker(&P) {}
4185
4186 LLVM_DUMP_METHOD void dump();
4187};
4188
4189struct VPlanIngredient {
4190 const Value *V;
4191
4192 VPlanIngredient(const Value *V) : V(V) {}
4193
4194 void print(raw_ostream &O) const;
4195};
4196
4197inline raw_ostream &operator<<(raw_ostream &OS, const VPlanIngredient &I) {
4198 I.print(OS);
4199 return OS;
4200}
4201
4202inline raw_ostream &operator<<(raw_ostream &OS, const VPlan &Plan) {
4203 Plan.print(OS);
4204 return OS;
4205}
4206#endif
4207
4208class VPInterleavedAccessInfo {
4209 DenseMap<VPInstruction *, InterleaveGroup<VPInstruction> *>
4210 InterleaveGroupMap;
4211
4212 /// Type for mapping of instruction based interleave groups to VPInstruction
4213 /// interleave groups
4214 using Old2NewTy = DenseMap<InterleaveGroup<Instruction> *,
4215 InterleaveGroup<VPInstruction> *>;
4216
4217 /// Recursively \p Region and populate VPlan based interleave groups based on
4218 /// \p IAI.
4219 void visitRegion(VPRegionBlock *Region, Old2NewTy &Old2New,
4220 InterleavedAccessInfo &IAI);
4221 /// Recursively traverse \p Block and populate VPlan based interleave groups
4222 /// based on \p IAI.
4223 void visitBlock(VPBlockBase *Block, Old2NewTy &Old2New,
4224 InterleavedAccessInfo &IAI);
4225
4226public:
4227 VPInterleavedAccessInfo(VPlan &Plan, InterleavedAccessInfo &IAI);
4228
4229 ~VPInterleavedAccessInfo() {
4230 SmallPtrSet<InterleaveGroup<VPInstruction> *, 4> DelSet;
4231 // Avoid releasing a pointer twice.
4232 for (auto &I : InterleaveGroupMap)
4233 DelSet.insert(I.second);
4234 for (auto *Ptr : DelSet)
4235 delete Ptr;
4236 }
4237
4238 /// Get the interleave group that \p Instr belongs to.
4239 ///
4240 /// \returns nullptr if doesn't have such group.
4241 InterleaveGroup<VPInstruction> *
4242 getInterleaveGroup(VPInstruction *Instr) const {
4243 return InterleaveGroupMap.lookup(Instr);
4244 }
4245};
4246
4247/// Class that maps (parts of) an existing VPlan to trees of combined
4248/// VPInstructions.
4249class VPlanSlp {
4250 enum class OpMode { Failed, Load, Opcode };
4251
4252 /// A DenseMapInfo implementation for using SmallVector<VPValue *, 4> as
4253 /// DenseMap keys.
4254 struct BundleDenseMapInfo {
4255 static SmallVector<VPValue *, 4> getEmptyKey() {
4256 return {reinterpret_cast<VPValue *>(-1)};
4257 }
4258
4259 static SmallVector<VPValue *, 4> getTombstoneKey() {
4260 return {reinterpret_cast<VPValue *>(-2)};
4261 }
4262
4263 static unsigned getHashValue(const SmallVector<VPValue *, 4> &V) {
4264 return static_cast<unsigned>(hash_combine_range(V.begin(), V.end()));
4265 }
4266
4267 static bool isEqual(const SmallVector<VPValue *, 4> &LHS,
4268 const SmallVector<VPValue *, 4> &RHS) {
4269 return LHS == RHS;
4270 }
4271 };
4272
4273 /// Mapping of values in the original VPlan to a combined VPInstruction.
4274 DenseMap<SmallVector<VPValue *, 4>, VPInstruction *, BundleDenseMapInfo>
4275 BundleToCombined;
4276
4277 VPInterleavedAccessInfo &IAI;
4278
4279 /// Basic block to operate on. For now, only instructions in a single BB are
4280 /// considered.
4281 const VPBasicBlock &BB;
4282
4283 /// Indicates whether we managed to combine all visited instructions or not.
4284 bool CompletelySLP = true;
4285
4286 /// Width of the widest combined bundle in bits.
4287 unsigned WidestBundleBits = 0;
4288
4289 using MultiNodeOpTy =
4290 typename std::pair<VPInstruction *, SmallVector<VPValue *, 4>>;
4291
4292 // Input operand bundles for the current multi node. Each multi node operand
4293 // bundle contains values not matching the multi node's opcode. They will
4294 // be reordered in reorderMultiNodeOps, once we completed building a
4295 // multi node.
4296 SmallVector<MultiNodeOpTy, 4> MultiNodeOps;
4297
4298 /// Indicates whether we are building a multi node currently.
4299 bool MultiNodeActive = false;
4300
4301 /// Check if we can vectorize Operands together.
4302 bool areVectorizable(ArrayRef<VPValue *> Operands) const;
4303
4304 /// Add combined instruction \p New for the bundle \p Operands.
4305 void addCombined(ArrayRef<VPValue *> Operands, VPInstruction *New);
4306
4307 /// Indicate we hit a bundle we failed to combine. Returns nullptr for now.
4308 VPInstruction *markFailed();
4309
4310 /// Reorder operands in the multi node to maximize sequential memory access
4311 /// and commutative operations.
4312 SmallVector<MultiNodeOpTy, 4> reorderMultiNodeOps();
4313
4314 /// Choose the best candidate to use for the lane after \p Last. The set of
4315 /// candidates to choose from are values with an opcode matching \p Last's
4316 /// or loads consecutive to \p Last.
4317 std::pair<OpMode, VPValue *> getBest(OpMode Mode, VPValue *Last,
4318 SmallPtrSetImpl<VPValue *> &Candidates,
4319 VPInterleavedAccessInfo &IAI);
4320
4321#if !defined(NDEBUG) || defined(LLVM_ENABLE_DUMP)
4322 /// Print bundle \p Values to dbgs().
4323 void dumpBundle(ArrayRef<VPValue *> Values);
4324#endif
4325
4326public:
4327 VPlanSlp(VPInterleavedAccessInfo &IAI, VPBasicBlock &BB) : IAI(IAI), BB(BB) {}
4328
4329 ~VPlanSlp() = default;
4330
4331 /// Tries to build an SLP tree rooted at \p Operands and returns a
4332 /// VPInstruction combining \p Operands, if they can be combined.
4333 VPInstruction *buildGraph(ArrayRef<VPValue *> Operands);
4334
4335 /// Return the width of the widest combined bundle in bits.
4336 unsigned getWidestBundleBits() const { return WidestBundleBits; }
4337
4338 /// Return true if all visited instruction can be combined.
4339 bool isCompletelySLP() const { return CompletelySLP; }
4340};
4341} // end namespace llvm
4342
4343#endif // LLVM_TRANSFORMS_VECTORIZE_VPLAN_H
